There’s a design flaw. Once an app has been approved by the parents, it’s treated as permanently approved. There’s no way to change that, only to block its usage. And here comes a second design flaw: the child can simply uninstall the app and reinstall it. They no longer need parental approval, and on top of that it bypasses all the restrictions such as screen-time limits, blocked apps, and so on. This is wonderful and has solved many of the tablet problems in our house. I have a few suggested feature additions to make it even better, Google:  1). Allow multiple shut-off & turn-on times during the day rather than just a simple bed-time schedule. That way we could allow morning use until it's time to leave for school and have it locked down until school's out.   2). Allow time limits for certain apps. Or exempt some apps from the time limit. For example, I might allow unlimited use for an educational app, but I might limit all other apps to 2 hours per day.  3). Allow a parent to send a message to the tablet, similar to a text message. "DINNER IN 10 MINUTES! SAVE YOUR GAME BECAUSE I'M ABOUT TO PUSH ***THE BUTTON***!"  4). Give the parent the option to add a pre-defined amount of time once limits have been reached rather than just hit "unlock." That way I can give my kids an extra 10 minutes without having to revisit it 10 minutes later.    Otherwise, parents - this is a great app and I highly recommend it! The fact that it's free is even better.

review_text: Long needed app. Very useful. We would always struggle to keep our 6 year old away from the screen: hard to peel him off in general and before bed, and even if we did he'd be bargaining again and again. It is now easy. Rules are set and device would lock once the total screentime is up or it's later than a certain pm time. I am seeing a better behavior already. He suddenly rediscovered his toys! Google please make option to count and control the TOTAL cumulative screentime on all devices on child's account, not just per device. Our kid has a tablet, but also uses our old phone when battery is depleted, hence we need to manually adjust the total allowed time.

Robust enough for a not-very-nerdy 11 year old. Limits work well on one device. Has a couple of odd drawbacks: 1. Daily limits have to be the same on all devices, and can't be shared between devices - so if your kid is allowed 2 hours per day on a phone and you give hir a notebook, they now have 4 screen hours per day. 2. Individual app time can be limited but they can't be grouped - there's no way to limit all games to an hour a day while allowing useful apps for longer.
